title: Add support for `.secret` Systemd units to Quadlet
body:
### Feature request description

Quadlet currently provides no equivalent for the `podman secret` command group. Adding support for `.secret` Systemd units to manage the life cycle of container secrets would be great. It greatly simplifies setups and would allow for implicit dependencies.

### Suggest potential solution

A `.secret` unit file might look like the following:

```
[Unit]
Description=My secrets, please don't look at them

[Secret]
SecretName=my_secret
Driver=file
File=/home/user/top_secret.txt
Label=Confidentiality=TopSecret
SecretDeleteOnStop=true
```

Declaring `Secret=my_secret.secret` in a `.container` unit could automatically pull in `Wants=my_secret-secret.service` and `After=my_secret-secret.service` dependencies, similar to `Network=my_network.network`.

`SecretDeleteOnStop` would behave similar to `NetworkDeleteOnStop`.

I'm undecided whether the driver opts of the `pass` and `shell` drivers should be declared using a common `DriverOpts=` directive (matching `podman secret create --driver-opts`), or whether Quadlet should add a directive for each driver optionÔÇª

Passing a secret via stdin naturally won't work. I don't think there's a reasonable use case for a `podman secret create --env` equivalent either.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

Creating custom Systemd units naturally still works, it's just cumbersome.

### Additional context

Off topic: Is there a reason why the `pass` driver options aren't documented? See https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/18387#issuecomment-1529816614

comments.body:
Thanks for opening this request. I've also been thinking about adding it. 

Regarding your open items:
> I'm undecided whether the driver opts of the pass and shell drivers should be declared using a common DriverOpts= directive (matching podman secret create --driver-opts), or whether Quadlet should add a directive for each driver option

I think it's better to just have `DriverOpts`. This way we don't have to maintain a list of options that may change in the future. It also makes it easier for the users to translate what they use in their `secret create` command into the Quadlet unit. The key should act like the `Label` key does. Meaning it can have multiple key=val (or just key) and can appear multiple times

> Passing a secret via stdin naturally won't work. I don't think there's a reasonable use case for a podman secret create --env equivalent either

I agree about `stdin`, but I don't see any harm in supporting `--env`. I think that almost everything we said "users won't want this" we eventually had to add. So, if we already thought about it, we should just support. I guess that then `File` and `Env` keys should be mutual exclusive. Using `Env` will both set `--env` and use the value.

I'll also add a question from my end:
What should we do with `--replace`? Keep in mind that unlike `network` or `volume`, `secret` does not have an `--ignore` flag. So, the command may fail if the secret exists.

> I think it's better to just have `DriverOpts`.

Good reasoning, I agree :+1:

> I agree about `stdin`, but I don't see any harm in supporting `--env`. [ÔÇª] I guess that then `File` and `Env` keys should be mutual exclusive. Using `Env` will both set `--env` and use the value.

Keep in mind that Systemd clears the environment by default, i.e. one must explicitly add env variables using the `Environment=` and `EnvironmentFile=` Systemd directives first. We'd end up with something like this:

```
[Unit]
Environment=FOO=bar

[Secret]
Environment=FOO
```

This feels cumbersome. It makes a little more sense with `EnvironmentFile=` because the variables are then read from a file, but since we can only create a single secret, this doesn't make much practical sense either IMHO.

However, this got me thinking: Since we don't support `stdin` we lack the possibility to create static secrets. So, how about adding a `Value=` directive that replaces `File=`? This `Value=` directive could then use the same format as the [`Environment=` Systemd directive](https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/systemd.exec.html#Environment=), just as Quadlet's `Environment=` directive for `.container` units uses that syntax. This practically also gives us env variable support (Quadlet unit `my_secret.secret`):

```
[Unit]
Environment=GREET=bar

[Secret]
Value="Hello $GREET"
```

Quadlet could then indeed use `--env` to nicely convert this into a Systemd unit `my_secret-secret.service`:

```
[Unit]
Environment=GREET=bar
Environment="SECRET_VALUE=Hello $GREET"

[Service]
ExecStart=podman secret create --env=true systemd-my_secret SECRET_VALUE
ÔÇª
```

Anyway, IMHO, this is nice-to-have, no must-have, so it could be added later.

> What should we do with `--replace`?

Good point.

Just to emphasize, neither `.network` nor `.volume` Quadlet units currently support `--ignore`.

The reason why there's no `--ignore` for `secret create`, but `--replace`, probably is that Podman secrets can be changed at runtime without affecting running containers. This isn't true for networks and volumes.

Anyway, I don't think that we need support for `--replace`. The default secret name should be Quadlet-specific, similar to e.g. `.network` units: Specifying no `NetworkName` results in the network being named `systemd-$name`, assuming exclusive management by the Quadlet unit. The same should be true for `.secret` units: Without a `SecretName` Quadlet should default to `systemd-$name`, again assuming exclusive management by the Quadlet unit. If one changes the secret, one restarts the Quadlet unit. If there's already a conflicting secret, network, or volume, the Quadlet unit fails to start.

I agree that using env can be cumbersome. But, that's not for us to decide. Someone will eventually ask for it and if we already have a way to implement it, I think we should take the simple way.

As for the `--ignore`, it's not about  `.network` and `.volume`  not supporting a key for it. They hard code `--ignore` into the `StartExec` command. The reason they do so is to avoid failing the unit when the resource already exits. This will sure happen if `SecretDeleteOnStop` is not `true`. But, it can also happen if it is, for example on hard power reset (which does not get to stop the units). 

Supporting `--replace` can allow changing the value of the secret by restarting the service (after changing the input file or env) without setting `SecretDeleteOnStop` to `true`

> I agree that using env can be cumbersome. But, that's not for us to decide. Someone will eventually ask for it and if we already have a way to implement it, I think we should take the simple way.

That's hard to document and even harder to understand from an user's perspective, because it requires the user to understand both the underlying `secret create` command (which IMHO shouldn't be assumed with Quadlet), and how Systemd handles environment variables. Adding a `Environment=` Quadlet directive will create a ton of user questions, because users won't understand how this interacts with Systemd's environment. It's just to complex and cumbersome. Thus it's unlikely to be used by a relevant number of users. The few users that do understand it and can't use one of the alternatives, can still create a custom Systemd service - unlike `run` with `--cidfile` et. al. it's easy to create fully portable Systemd services with `secret create`, i.e. there's a viable alternative to a `.secret` unit.

IMHO it really just doesn't make much sense to implement it that way - it should rather be either with a much simpler `Value=` directive hiding the complexity, or not at all. Expecting a unit like the following just to create a secret with a static value just doesn't make much sense IMHO:

```
[Unit]
Environment="VALUE=Please keep my secret safe"

[Secret]
Environment=VALUE
```

> As for the `--ignore`, it's not about `.network` and `.volume` not supporting a key for it. They hard code `--ignore` into the `StartExec` command. The reason they do so is to avoid failing the unit when the resource already exits.

HmmÔÇª I see. Hard coding `--replace` alike is very problematic IMO, because it's doing something else: `--ignore` simply makes it a no-op in case the network/volume exists, but `--replace` might unexpectedly change the value.

The same behaviour as `--ignore` could be achieved with a preceding `secret exists`, also fixing `systemctl restart` without `SecretDeleteOnStop`. `--replace` could be used for `systemctl reload` though. This IMO also better matches Systemd's logic of `restart` vs. `reload`.

Or, just add `--ignore` to `secret create`.

I agree that using `Environment` does not make sense. Not sure that `EnvironmentFile` doesn't. But, I don't feel the need to insist. Let's get something rolling and add it if and when.

The `--ignore` part is a bigger issue. I agree that we shouldn't hardcode `--replace`. I'm not a fan of of prepending the command with `secret exists` though it will do the trick.
I've opened a PR for `--ignore` flag: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26467
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
This discussion thread might be related: https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/26762
Is there any update on this topic? How can the community support here?
